w:r.w:t: HP Victus · RTX 4050 · 16GB RAM · 24-Hour Hackathon Build
Includes: VRAM Management · Token Optimization · Hallucination Defense · Drift Control · Guardrails · 5 Trap Mitigations · Full Build Methodology
The following represents the complete end-to-end pipeline from user input to packaged content output, with all guards, fallbacks, and quality gates embedded inline.
ZIP DL UI Preview Slack notify
Streamlit brand score Block Kit msg
All tools are free and open-source. Two API keys required (Groq + Fal.ai) — both have free tiers — kept as fallbacks only. Primary path is 100% local.
Python 3.11 — entire stack. Single language, fastest hackathon iteration.
venv with frozen requirements.txt. MUST be locked pre-hackathon (Trap 5). Never install new packages during event.
CUDA 12.1 — RTX 4050 Ada Lovelace. Verify with nvidia-smi before hackathon.
Streamlit 1.35 — zero HTML/CSS, rapid iteration, native streaming support via st.write_stream.
st.session_state — ALL generated data stored here immediately. Never in local variables. Initialize ALL keys with defaults at app top. (Trap 2 mitigation)
st.spinner blocking (synchronous) — eliminates Celery complexity since image gen is API call (3-5s). No polling loop needed.
FastAPI — StreamingResponse for SSE, Pydantic models, CORS middleware. Uvicorn with --timeout-keep-alive 120 (Trap 3 mitigation).
LangChain 0.2 — PromptTemplate, with_structured_output(Pydantic), chain.astream() async generator. (Improvement 1)
REMOVED for hackathon — synchronous image generation via st.spinner. Add Celery post-hackathon for multi-user scale.
REMOVED for hackathon — no Celery means no broker needed. Eliminates entire dependency category.
Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3 Q4 via Ollama — 4.1GB VRAM, ~30 tok/s on RTX 4050. keep_alive=0 mandatory. seed=42 for determinism.
Groq API → Llama 3.1-8B-Instruct. ChatGroq LangChain wrapper. Toggle: LLM_PROVIDER=ollama|groq in .env.
llm_creative (temp=0.75) for copy generation. llm_structured (temp=0.1) for JSON formatting pass. (Improvement 5)
max_tokens=1200 on all Ollama calls — prevents runaway generation. (Token Optimization 4)
ChromaDB — local persistent, in-process, zero setup. Two collections: 'posts' and 'brand'.
sentence-transformers all-MiniLM-L6-v2 — 90MB, CPU inference ~200ms. Pre-warmed on startup.
Top-3 results (not 5) filtered by platform AND engagement_score ≥ 0.6. (Improvement 3, Token Optimization 1)
Posts truncated to 80 tokens (320 chars) at ingestion. Embedding captures full semantics, prompt gets compressed version. (Token Optimization 1)
2 high-engagement examples (positive) + 1 low-engagement example with annotation (negative contrast). (Improvement 2)

RTX 4050 has 6GB VRAM. Mistral-7B Q4 = 4.1GB. SDXL-Turbo fp16 = 6.8GB (DOES NOT FIT). SDXL-Turbo int8 = 4.2GB (fits after Mistral unloads). STRICT SEQUENTIAL EXECUTION: LLM finishes → keep_alive=0 → gc.collect() → torch.cuda.empty_cache() → torch.cuda.synchronize() → 4s sleep → SDXL loads.
SDXL-Turbo via HuggingFace diffusers with bitsandbytes int8 quantization. ~4.2GB VRAM, ~15-25s generation. Runs AFTER LLM fully unloads.
Fal.ai API ($10 free credits, ~$0.003/image). Toggle: IMAGE_PROVIDER=local|api in .env. Zero VRAM.
Pollinations.ai — no API key, no signup, completely free. Unreliable SLA but works.
Pillow — composite color band + logo + CTA text on generated image. CPU only.
xformers==0.0.24 — reduces SDXL VRAM by ~15%. MUST match torch CUDA version exactly. (Trap 5)
Try/except around SDXL load — if OOM occurs, automatically switch to fal.ai silently. User sees image either way.
Pillow — 5 slides at 1080×1080px. Semi-transparent overlay behind all text (alpha=180). Hard char limits: title ≤40 chars, body ≤80 chars enforced in LLM prompt AND renderer. (Issue 3 / Pillow fix)
imageio.mimsave() — stitch 5 carousel slides at 0.6fps. 10 lines of code. Fulfills GIF requirement. (Issue 4)
imageio with mp4 writer — same slides as 5-second video. Fulfills short video requirement. Zero VRAM. (Issue 4)
Google Fonts TTF downloaded locally pre-hackathon. Inter or Poppins. Never download during event.
SQLite — file-based, zero setup, sufficient for single-user hackathon. Stores: feature_name, timestamp, brand_score, token_count, generation_time, platforms, zip_path.
mock_events.json (3 events), mock_trends.json (5 trends/platform), brand_guidelines.json (tone, forbidden words, competitors, hashtag whitelist, tone examples with counter-examples).
seed_posts.json — 30 posts (10/platform) with engagement_score, tone, mood_tags, platform metadata. High-scoring posts represent ideal brand voice.
Local /output/{session_id}/ directory. ZIPs served via FileResponse. Cleaned up after 24h.
json-repair library — auto-fixes ~80% of malformed LLM JSON output. Install: pip install json-repair. Non-optional.
tiktoken — count assembled prompt tokens before every LLM call. Reduce RAG k if approaching 3,000 token soft limit.
Custom services/input_sanitizer.py — regex strip injection patterns, delimiter wrapping, length validation.
services/content_validator.py — hallucination scan (regex for stats), competitor check, forbidden words, platform similarity, hashtag format.
services/brand_scorer.py — cosine similarity vs top-3 ChromaDB posts + keyword bonus/penalty layer. Tiers: ≥80 pass, 65-79 warn, <65 silent regen.
Python stdlib zipfile — no extra dependency. Per-platform directory structure with manifest.json.
slack-sdk WebhookClient — Block Kit message with feature name, brand score, platforms, download link.
LangChain callback metadata — log input_tokens, output_tokens, generation_time_ms, brand_score, retries_used to SQLite. Display in Streamlit sidebar stats expander. (Improvement 6)
LOCK THESE VERSIONS BEFORE THE HACKATHON — DO NOT UPDATE DURING EVENT
Test every version below in a fresh venv. Run SDXL-Turbo benchmark (must complete in <25s). Freeze exact versions with pip freeze > requirements.txt. Never install new packages during the hackathon.
torch==2.2.0+cu121 # CUDA build — install via --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121
xformers==0.0.24 # VRAM reduction — optional but recommended

Streamlit form: feature_name (text_input, max 100 chars), description (text_area, max 500 chars with live counter), target_audience (text_input), tone_selector (selectbox: Professional/Witty/Minimal/Excited/Authoritative), platform_checkboxes (multiselect), launch_date (optional). All required fields validated before submit button activates.

LangChain with_structured_output(GeneratedCopy Pydantic model). Mistral-7B primary. Retrieves top-3 ChromaDB posts filtered by platform + engagement_score ≥ 0.6. Two LLM instances: llm_creative (temp=0.75) + llm_structured (temp=0.1). seed=42 for demo determinism.

Pure Python PlatformAdapter class. LinkedIn: ≤1300 chars truncated at sentence boundary, 3-5 hashtags. X: ≤280 chars, 2-3 hashtags, optional thread split. Instagram: ≤2200 chars, 20-30 hashtags in separate comment block. Constraint repeated at point of use in prompt (constraint anchoring).

FastAPI async StreamingResponse using chain.astream(). Uvicorn --timeout-keep-alive 120. Streamlit httpx.stream() consumer updating st.empty() placeholder per token. Tokens routed to platform-specific st.tabs as JSON keys are parsed. (Trap 3 fix)

Separate rewrite-only LLM call: ~700 tokens total (vs 2,100 for full regen). Input: original_copy + alternate_tone. Output: rewritten copy only. Displays side-by-side with tone label. Brand score computed for both — shows which scores higher.

Cosine similarity vs top-3 high-engagement ChromaDB posts (not raw guidelines). Keyword bonus (+10 if required tone words present), penalty (-15 if forbidden words found, -10 if competitor names found). Tiers: ≥80 green Verified, 65-79 amber Review, <65 red auto-regen at temp=0.5.

Dedicated lightweight image prompt LLM call (~130 tokens total) AFTER main generation. Uses llm_creative at temp=0.9. Runs after: keep_alive=0 → gc.collect() → cuda.empty_cache() → synchronize() → 4s sleep → SDXL-Turbo int8 loads. OOM auto-fallback to fal.ai. Pillow brand overlay: color band + logo + CTA.

LLM outputs 5-slide JSON structure. Pillow renders at 1080×1080px. Semi-transparent dark rect (alpha=180) behind all text. Hard char limits enforced: title ≤40 chars, body ≤80 chars. Slide number + logo watermark. Platform resize: LinkedIn 1200×628, Instagram 1080×1080.
imageio.mimsave('carousel.gif', frames, fps=0.6, loop=0). 10 lines of code. Fulfills problem statement GIF requirement. Added to ZIP bundle under /animations/. (Issue 4 fix — free rubric points)
imageio mp4 writer — same 5 slides as 5-second video. Fulfills short video requirement. Zero VRAM. Together with GIF: both format requirements covered by pure CPU Pillow slides. (Issue 4 fix)

Streamlit selectbox. Each tone defined with 3 positive examples + 3 counter-examples in brand_guidelines.json. Both injected into system prompt when tone selected. Grounds abstract label in concrete language. (Drift Type 4 fix)

Sidebar button reads next item from mock_events.json via GET /mock-events. Populates form fields via session_state. Server-side pointer advances on each call (stored in SQLite). Green pulsing 'Scraper running' indicator in sidebar. Sells full automation vision.

mock_trends.json — 5 trends per platform with keyword, score, volume, suggested_angle. Injected into LLM prompt as 'currently trending context'. Trending topics panel shown as st.expander above form. Dynamic output schema: only injects trends for selected platforms. (Token Optimization 2)

ChromaDB seed_posts.json includes engagement_score (0.0-1.0, varied). RAG retriever sorts by score desc. UI shows 'Learning from X past posts — avg score Y'. Feedback loop architecture diagram in History tab.

PackageBuilder creates /output/{session_id}/: /linkedin/post.txt, /twitter/post.txt+thread.txt, /instagram/caption.txt+hashtags.txt, /images/hero_*.png (per platform size), /carousel/slide_01-05.png, /animations/carousel.gif+launch.mp4, manifest.json with all scores and metadata.
slack-sdk WebhookClient. Block Kit message: feature name, brand score, platforms, token stats, download note. Triggered on package completion and on-demand via UI button.
SQLite table: feature_name, timestamp, brand_score, input_tokens, output_tokens, generation_time_ms, retries_used, platforms, zip_path. Last 10 shown in History tab with re-download.

After every generation: log and display input_tokens, output_tokens, total_time_ms, brand_score, retries_used. Shown in sidebar stats expander. Pitch narrative: 'Generated 1,847 tokens across 3 platforms in 34s — Brand score 91/100.' (Improvement 6)

Pydantic ValidationResult model returned alongside copy: score, tier, hallucination_flags, competitor_flags, forbidden_flags, similarity_flags, regenerated bool. Each field rendered in UI with appropriate badge/warning.

Main working screen. Two-column layout: left (input form + mock scraper + trends panel), right (live streaming output in platform sub-tabs + brand score meter + A/B toggle + compliance badges + validation card).
Hero image with generation progress spinner. Carousel 5-slide horizontal preview. GIF preview. MP4 download. Per-platform image downloads. Full ZIP download. Slack notify button.
Last 10 generations table (score, tokens, time, re-download). Full system architecture diagram image. Feedback loop and scraper vision explained. Used for pitch narrative.
4.2 Session State — Complete Key List (Trap 2 Fix)
Initialize ALL keys at app top before any widget. Store ALL generated data in session_state BEFORE any st.rerun() call. Never store generated content in local variables.
# Initialize at top of ui/app.py — BEFORE any widget
st.text_input, max_chars=100. Placeholder: 'e.g. Dark Mode, AI Autocomplete'. Required field.
st.text_area, height=120, max_chars=500. Live counter via st.caption. Hard 500-char limit prevents context overflow.
st.text_input. Placeholder: 'e.g. enterprise teams, student developers'.
st.selectbox: Professional/Witty/Minimal/Excited/Authoritative. Each tone loads 3 examples + 3 counter-examples from brand_guidelines.json into system prompt.
st.multiselect: LinkedIn/X-Twitter/Instagram. Default all selected. Drives dynamic output schema — only generates selected platforms.
st.expander '📈 Currently trending (simulated)'. Shows 5 topics per selected platform with score bars from mock_trends.json.
st.sidebar button '⚡ New event detected'. Fetches /mock-events, writes fields to session_state, calls st.rerun(). Shows mock_events.json item cycling through 3 pre-written events.

st.button '🚀 Generate Content Package'. Disabled until feature_name and description are non-empty. On click: set session_state.generating=True, call /generate, store session_id in session_state IMMEDIATELY.

st.empty() placeholder updated per SSE token chunk. httpx.stream() consumer iterates response.iter_text(). Tokens accumulated per platform JSON key. When key complete: render in respective platform tab.
st.tabs(['LinkedIn','X / Twitter','Instagram']). Each tab shows: formatted copy, hashtags list, CTA, compliance badge (char count + hashtag count).
st.metric('Brand Alignment', f'{score}/100'). st.progress(score/100). CSS injection colors bar: green ≥80, amber 65-79, red <65. Sub-scores: tone match, keyword compliance, forbidden word check.

Rendered from ValidationResult Pydantic model. Shows: hallucination_flags as inline ⚠️ warnings, competitor_flags as 🔴 rewrite notices, forbidden_flags as replacement suggestions, similarity_flags as 'platforms too similar' warnings, regenerated badge if auto-improved.
st.expander 'Generate A/B variant (alternate tone)'. Single button call triggers rewrite-only ~700 token call. Shows side-by-side in st.columns. Both scores compared.
st.expander '📊 Generation stats'. Shows: input_tokens, output_tokens, generation_time_ms, retries_used, brand_score. Formatted for judge presentation.

Accepts FeatureInput Pydantic model. Runs: input_sanitizer → token_count_check → rag_chain.astream() → content_validator → brand_scorer. Returns: StreamingResponse (SSE) with token chunks + final event containing session_id, brand_score, validation_result.

Accepts ImageGenRequest (session_id, prompt). Runs SEQUENTIAL after LLM: gc.collect() + cuda.empty_cache() + sleep(4) + SDXL-Turbo int8. Returns synchronously (blocking with st.spinner on frontend). OOM auto-fallback to fal.ai.
Accepts VariantRequest (original_copy, original_tone, variant_tone). Rewrite-only prompt ~700 tokens. Returns PlatformCopy for alternate tone + brand_score.
Assembles ZIP via PackageBuilder. Returns FileResponse(zip_path, media_type='application/zip').
Returns last 10 SQLite generation records as JSON array.
Returns next mock_events.json item. Advances server-side pointer (stored in SQLite). Cycles through 3 events.
Returns mock_trends.json. Decoupled endpoint — replace with real API call post-hackathon without changing frontend.
Returns {'status':'ok', 'ollama': bool, 'chromadb': bool, 'sdxl_loaded': bool}. Used for startup verification and demo confidence check.

sanitize(text) — strip injection patterns (ignore/system/forget regex), length validate, return cleaned text or raise HTTPException 422. wrap_with_delimiters(text) — encloses user content in ---DATA START/END--- markers. count_tokens(prompt) — tiktoken count, raise if >3000.

seed_from_json(path) — on startup, embed and upsert 30 seed posts with full metadata. retrieve_similar(query, platform, k=3) — filtered by platform + engagement_score ≥ 0.6, sorted by score desc. add_post(text, metadata) — for real feedback loop post-hackathon. pre_warm() — dummy embed call on startup to load model into RAM.

build_prompt(feature_input, retrieved_docs, trends, brand) — assembles PromptTemplate. Dynamic output schema based on selected_platforms. Constraint anchoring: platform limits repeated at point of generation. invoke_with_retry(prompt, max_retries=2) — json_repair on parse fail, LLM retry on second fail, graceful fallback on third.

adapt_linkedin(text, hashtags) — ≤1300 chars at sentence boundary, 3-5 tags. adapt_twitter(text, hashtags) — ≤280 chars, 2-3 tags, optional thread split at 3 logical points. adapt_instagram(text, hashtags) — ≤2200 chars, 20-30 tags in separate string for first comment. Each returns PlatformCopy Pydantic model with compliant bool.

check_hallucinations(text) — regex: \d+%, \d+ million/billion/users. Returns flagged claims list. check_competitors(text) — scan against competitors list, returns sentences for rewrite. check_forbidden_words(text) — case-insensitive, returns positions + replacement suggestions. check_platform_similarity(posts) — pairwise cosine, flag pairs >0.85. validate_hashtags(tags) — format + whitelist check.

generate_hero(prompt, session_id) — sequential VRAM management: keep_alive=0 call → gc.collect() → cuda.empty_cache() → synchronize() → sleep(4) → load pipeline → generate → unload. OOM except → fal_client fallback. apply_brand_overlay(image) — Pillow composite. resize_for_platform(image, platform) — crop/resize to platform spec.

render_slides(structure, brand_config) — 5×1080×1080 PNGs. Semi-transparent dark rect alpha=180 behind text. Hard truncate: title[:40], body[:80] as safety net. Logo watermark. generate_gif(slides) — imageio.mimsave fps=0.6. generate_mp4(slides) — imageio mp4 writer 5s.

pre_compute_anchor() — on startup, compute embedding of top-3 high-score ChromaDB posts as anchor. score(copy_text) — cosine_sim × 100 + keyword_bonus - forbidden_penalty. score_and_gate(text) — if <65: regenerate at temp=0.5, return new score + regenerated=True flag.

build(session_id, copy, images, scores) — creates directory tree, writes all text files, copies image files, writes manifest.json. create_zip(session_id) — zipfile.ZipFile compresses entire directory. Manifest includes: all scores, token stats, platform list, generation timestamp, model versions.
send_slack(feature_name, score, platforms, token_stats) — Block Kit message. Configurable via SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL env var. Called on package completion and on-demand.
title: str = Field(max_length=40) # enforced by LLM prompt AND validator
body: str = Field(max_length=80) # enforced by LLM prompt AND validator
│ └── startup.py # pre-warm embeddings, seed ChromaDB, health check
│ ├── seed_posts.json # 30 seeded posts with engagement scores

Complete Steps 1-15 (core pipeline end-to-end) before touching any other feature. A working demo of just the core beats a broken demo of everything. The Trap pre-checks in Phase 0 are mandatory — if any fail, stop and fix before the hackathon starts.
PHASE 0 — Pre-Hackathon Validation (Must Complete Before Event)
These are not optional. If any of these fail during the hackathon, you lose hours. Do them today.

Run: nvidia-smi — confirm RTX 4050, Driver ≥525, CUDA 12.1. Run: python -c "import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available(), torch.version.cuda, torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))" — must print True, 12.1, NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4050. On Windows: run 'where nvcc' — if not found, add CUDA toolkit to system PATH. This is the single most common silent failure on Windows.
Lock requirements.txt and test SDXL-Turbo benchmark (Trap 5)

Create fresh venv. Install exact versions from Section 2.11. Run test_sdxl_bench.py: load SDXL-Turbo int8, generate one 1024x1024 image with num_inference_steps=4, time it — MUST complete in under 25 seconds. If slower: xformers not active, CUDA not configured, or int8 not loading. Fix this now. Freeze with: pip freeze > requirements.txt. Never install new packages during the event.
Pre-download all models (Trap 1 — conference WiFi protection)

Run: ollama pull mistral — verify ~/.ollama/models has ~4.1GB. Run SDXL-Turbo download script — verify ~/.cache/huggingface has ~6.9GB. Run SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2') once — cached. Download TTF fonts to data/fonts/. Total pre-hackathon model storage: ~11.2GB. None of this downloads during the event.

Write docker-compose.yml with volume mounts: ~/.ollama:/root/.ollama and ~/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface. Run docker-compose build — image should be ~800MB (Python+libs only, no models). Run docker-compose up and verify inside container: import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available()) prints True. If False on Windows WSL2: enable Docker Desktop GPU support in settings.

Build a 30-line Streamlit app: button sets session_state.task_id='test123', calls a dummy 15-second delay via requests.get('/dummy-task'), polls /task-status every 2s with time.sleep(2) BEFORE st.rerun(). During the 15-second wait: click other buttons, interact with widgets. Confirm task_id stays in session_state and polling continues. If this breaks: you need to rethink async architecture before building.
Test FastAPI SSE → Streamlit streaming micro-prototype (Trap 3)

Build minimal FastAPI endpoint: async def token_generator() yields 'word ' every 0.1s for 30 seconds wrapped in StreamingResponse. Build minimal Streamlit consumer: httpx.stream('GET', url) iterating response.iter_text(), updating st.empty() placeholder. Start uvicorn with --timeout-keep-alive 120. Confirm tokens render one-by-one. Confirm connection survives 60 seconds. If blank screen: requests not in stream mode. If timeout at 5s: missing keepalive flag.
Write start.sh non-Docker fallback (Trap 1 safety net)

Write start.sh: uvicorn app.main:app --port 8000 --timeout-keep-alive 120 & sleep 3 && streamlit run ui/app.py --server.port 8501 &. Test it works standalone. This is your demo fallback if Docker GPU passthrough fails at the venue. Having this ready means Docker is a nice-to-have presentation choice, not a single point of failure.
Pre-generate 2 complete demo outputs and save as static files

Generate a complete content package for 'Dark Mode' feature and one for 'AI Autocomplete'. Save all output files: copy JSON, hero images, carousel PNGs, GIF, MP4, ZIP. Store in data/demo_cache/. If everything fails during live demo: pull up these pre-generated outputs, walk judges through the content, explain the system architecture confidently. A polished static demo beats a broken live demo every time.

Create the full directory tree from Section 5.4. Add __init__.py to each Python package. Create .env with: LLM_PROVIDER=ollama, IMAGE_PROVIDER=local, OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434, SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL=placeholder. Create requirements.txt from Section 2.11. This skeleton is the foundation — getting it right now prevents import errors during the hackathon.

Structure: brand_name, voice_description (2-3 sentences), tone_words (array of adjectives), forbidden_words (array), competitors (array for hallucination check), color_primary hex, color_secondary hex, tagline, logo_path, required_cta_phrases, tone_definitions object (each tone: {label, examples: [3 strings], counter_examples: [3 strings]}). This file drives the system prompt, brand scoring anchor, forbidden word detection, competitor blocking, and tone grounding.

Array of 30 objects: 10 LinkedIn, 10 X/Twitter, 10 Instagram. Each: text (truncated to 320 chars), platform, tone (matches tone selector options), feature_type, engagement_score (0.0-1.0, varied — make top-third clearly higher quality), mood_tags array, timestamp. Include 3 intentionally low-scoring posts per platform (for contrast teaching in RAG). High-engagement posts must represent ideal brand voice — these are what the LLM will pattern-match against.

mock_events.json: array of 3 realistic product feature events with feature_name, description, target_audience, launch_date. Each should be a different feature type (UI, performance, integration). mock_trends.json: object with linkedin/twitter/instagram keys. Each has array of 5 items: {keyword, score (0-100), volume ('High'/'Medium'/'Low'), suggested_angle (one sentence)}. Write trends that sound current and relevant to your target company's domain.

In services/memory_service.py: create ChromaDB PersistentClient at data/chroma_db/. Create 'posts' collection and 'brand' collection. seed_from_json(): read seed_posts.json, for each post: truncate to 320 chars for document text, embed full text with SentenceTransformer, upsert with complete metadata including engagement_score. Load brand_guidelines.json voice_description + tone_words concatenated into 'brand' collection. pre_warm(): call embed(['warmup']) to load model into RAM. Write a test: retrieve_similar('new feature announcement', 'linkedin', k=3) — confirm 3 results returned with correct metadata.

In services/input_sanitizer.py: sanitize(text) strips patterns matching r'(ignore|forget|disregard).*(previous|above|instructions)' case-insensitive, r'(system:|assistant:|<system>)', r'[<>]{3,}'. Length validate. wrap_with_delimiters(text) wraps in ---DATA START---/---DATA END---. count_tokens(prompt_string) uses tiktoken.get_encoding('cl100k_base').encode(). Raise HTTPException(422) on injection detection with user-facing message. Test with a deliberate injection string — confirm it's stripped before reaching LLM.
Design and implement the master prompt template

In services/rag_chain.py: PromptTemplate with sections: (1) System — brand voice_description, tone instruction with examples+counter-examples from brand_guidelines.json, forbidden words list, 'Do NOT invent statistics/numbers/user counts — only use facts from feature description'. (2) Examples — 2 high-engagement posts labeled '✓ Good example' + 1 low-engagement labeled '✗ Avoid this style'. (3) Trends — top-3 per selected platform from mock_trends.json. (4) Feature input — wrapped in delimiters. (5) Output schema — dynamic based on selected platforms only, with char constraints repeated at point of generation. Use with_structured_output(GeneratedCopy) for schema enforcement. Test prompt token count with tiktoken — confirm under 1,200 input tokens.
Implement RAGChain with two LLM instances and retry logic

llm_creative = OllamaLLM(model='mistral', temperature=0.75, seed=42, keep_alive=0, num_predict=1200). llm_structured = OllamaLLM(model='mistral', temperature=0.1, seed=42, keep_alive=0, num_predict=400). Chain: llm_creative generates copy content → llm_structured formats as valid JSON. invoke_with_retry(input, max_retries=2): wrap json.loads in try/except → call json_repair on fail → LLM retry with 'Fix this JSON, return only valid JSON: [broken]' → if second retry fails: return graceful_fallback_dict with raw text in 'raw_output' field. Log retry count to token_stats. Test: deliberately corrupt JSON and confirm repair works.

In services/platform_adapter.py: adapt_linkedin(text, hashtags): find last period before char 1300, truncate there, keep 3-5 hashtags, ensure CTA on last line, set compliant=True. adapt_twitter(text, hashtags): truncate at 275 chars at sentence boundary, keep 2-3 hashtags, if len>140 split into thread at 3 logical points (find paragraph breaks). adapt_instagram(text, hashtags): keep 2200 chars, separate 20-30 hashtags into hashtags_comment field, verify at least one emoji present. All methods return PlatformCopy Pydantic model. Test each method with edge cases: text exactly at limit, text 3× over limit, no hashtags provided.
Implement FastAPI /generate endpoint with async streaming (Trap 3 fix)

In app/main.py: async def generate(feature: FeatureInput): (1) sanitize all string inputs. (2) count tokens, reduce RAG k if needed. (3) retrieve RAG docs from MemoryService. (4) load brand context, trends. (5) build prompt. (6) define async def token_generator(): async for chunk in rag_chain.astream(prompt_input): yield f'data: {chunk}\n\n'. (7) return StreamingResponse(token_generator(), media_type='text/event-stream', headers={'X-Accel-Buffering':'no', 'Cache-Control':'no-cache'}). Add FastAPI startup event calling MemoryService.pre_warm() and a dummy embed call. Add CORS middleware for Streamlit origin.

In services/content_validator.py: check_hallucinations(text): regex patterns r'\d+\s*%', r'\d+[,.]?\d*\s*(million|billion|thousand|users|customers|downloads)', r'over\s+\d+'. Returns list of flagged substrings with positions. check_competitors(text): case-insensitive search for each name in brand_guidelines.competitors list. Returns list of sentences containing competitor names. check_forbidden_words(text): case-insensitive scan of brand_guidelines.forbidden_words. check_platform_similarity(posts_dict): embed each platform's caption, compute pairwise cosine_similarity, flag pairs >0.85. validate_hashtags(tags): each tag must match r'^#[A-Za-z0-9_]+$', length 2-30 chars, checked against whitelist if provided.

In services/brand_scorer.py: pre_compute_anchor() on startup: retrieve top-3 posts by engagement_score from ChromaDB, embed their concatenated text, store as brand_anchor_embedding module variable. score(copy_text): embed copy_text, cosine_similarity(copy_emb, brand_anchor_emb) * 100. Add: +10 if all brand_guidelines.tone_words found in text (case-insensitive), -15 if any forbidden_words found, -10 if any competitor names found, +5 if required CTA phrase present. Cap at 0-100. score_and_gate(text, generation_fn): if score < 65: call generation_fn with temperature=0.5 silently, rescore, return new_copy + new_score + regenerated=True. Return ValidationResult Pydantic model.
Implement A/B variant endpoint and token logging

POST /generate-variant: accepts VariantRequest(original_copy, original_tone, variant_tone). Build rewrite-only prompt: 'Rewrite the following marketing post in a {variant_tone} tone. Keep identical facts and CTA. Output ONLY the rewritten text, no explanation.' (~50 input tokens). Append original_copy (~300 tokens). Total input ~350 tokens + ~400 output = ~750 tokens vs 2,100 for full regen. Return PlatformCopy + brand_score. Token logging: create TokenStats Pydantic model, populate from LangChain callback metadata (use get_num_tokens or response.usage), save to SQLite generation_stats table, return in API response for Streamlit display.
Implement ImageService with VRAM sequential management (Issue 1 fix)

In services/image_service.py: module-level pipeline = None (lazy load). generate_hero(prompt, session_id): (1) assert Ollama is not running: call ollama API keep_alive=0 endpoint. (2) gc.collect(). (3) torch.cuda.empty_cache(). (4) torch.cuda.synchronize(). (5) time.sleep(4). (6) load pipeline with load_in_8bit=True, enable_attention_slicing(). (7) generate image with num_inference_steps=4, guidance_scale=0.0. (8) delete pipeline, gc.collect(), cuda.empty_cache() again. Wrap in try/except RuntimeError as OOM: if 'out of memory' in str(e): fall back to fal_client.run('fal-ai/fast-sdxl', arguments={'prompt': prompt}). Monitor with nvidia-smi watch during testing.

In services/image_service.py, apply_brand_overlay(pil_image, brand_config): (1) Draw 60px color band at bottom using brand_config['color_primary'] with ImageDraw.rectangle. (2) Load logo PNG with Image.open, convert to RGBA for transparency. Resize to 80×80. Paste at bottom-right corner (image.width-90, image.height-70). (3) Load brand font from data/fonts/. Draw CTA text in white at bottom-center using ImageDraw.text. (4) resize_for_platform(image, platform): LinkedIn: resize to 1200×628 with center crop. Instagram: already 1024×1024, resize to 1080×1080. Return modified PIL Image.
Implement CarouselService with Pillow and imageio (Issue 3 + Issue 4 fix)

In services/carousel_service.py: render_slides(structure): for each of 5 slides: (1) Image.new('RGB', (1080,1080), brand_config['color_secondary']). (2) ImageDraw.rectangle accent band top 120px in color_primary. (3) semi-transparent overlay: create RGBA image same size, draw dark rect (0,0,0,180) in text area, Image.alpha_composite. (4) title = slide.title[:40] — HARD truncation. body = slide.body[:80] — HARD truncation. Draw title (bold 52pt) and body (regular 36pt) with ImageDraw.text. (5) Slide number small bottom-right. Logo watermark bottom-left 15% opacity. Save as slide_0N.png. generate_gif(slide_paths): imageio.mimsave(gif_path, [np.array(Image.open(p)) for p in slide_paths], fps=0.6, loop=0). generate_mp4(slide_paths): imageio.get_writer(mp4_path, fps=1) context manager, append each frame.
Build Streamlit app skeleton with complete session_state init (Trap 2 fix)

In ui/app.py: st.set_page_config(layout='wide', page_title='MarketAI', page_icon='🚀'). Load brand_guidelines.json at top for brand name in header. Initialize ALL session_state keys from Section 4.2 — every single one, with defaults. Create tab1, tab2, tab3 = st.tabs(['🚀 Generate', '🎨 Visuals', '📊 History']). Build left/right column layout in tab1 with st.columns([1, 1.4]). This skeleton must be complete before adding any widgets.
Build left column form and mock scraper trigger

All widgets in left column. Feature name, description (with live char counter via st.caption(f'{len(desc)}/500')), audience, tone selectbox, platform multiselect. Trending topics expander reads mock_trends.json filtered to selected platforms. Mock scraper button: on click → fetch GET /mock-events → write feature_name, description, target_audience to session_state keys → call st.rerun(). Note: use st.session_state to pre-populate text inputs using session_state keys as their 'value' parameter — this is how Streamlit form pre-fill works. Generate button: disabled if feature_name or description empty, checked via st.button disabled= parameter.
Wire live streaming output with SSE consumption (Trap 3 final implementation)

In right column: on Generate click → (1) st.session_state.generating = True. (2) Assemble feature_input dict. (3) Use httpx.stream('POST', FASTAPI_URL+'/generate', json=feature_input, timeout=120) context manager. (4) Create platform text accumulators. (5) Iterate response.iter_text(). (6) Parse SSE chunks: split on 'data: ', strip. (7) Accumulate tokens per platform key as JSON streams. (8) Update st.empty() placeholders per platform tab on each chunk. (9) On final SSE event: parse session_id, brand_score, validation_result, token_stats from JSON. (10) Store all in session_state. (11) Set session_state.generating=False. (12) Call st.rerun() for final render.
Build brand score meter and validation card UI

After generation: st.metric('Brand Alignment', f'{score}/100', delta=None). Inject custom CSS via st.markdown('<style>...</style>', unsafe_allow_html=True) to color progress bar: green #1D9E75 if score≥80, amber #BA7517 if 65-79, red #E24B4A if <65. Sub-scores in st.columns(3). Render ValidationResult: loop hallucination_flags → st.warning(f'⚠️ Unverified stat: {flag}'). Loop competitor_flags → st.error(f'🔴 Competitor mention — auto-rewriting...'). Loop forbidden_flags → st.warning(f'Forbidden word: {flag}'). If regenerated=True: st.info(f'🔄 Auto-improved — original score: {original_score}/100'). Token stats expander: formatted table of all TokenStats fields.

Tab 2: call POST /generate-image with session_id — wrap in st.spinner('🎨 Generating visuals — ~20 seconds'). On return: display hero image with st.image. Horizontal carousel: st.columns(5) showing each slide thumbnail. Download buttons for each asset. Full ZIP download via st.download_button reading FileResponse from /package/{session_id}. Slack button calls POST /notify. Tab 3: fetch GET /history, display as st.dataframe with columns: feature, score, tokens, time, platforms, download. Below: st.image('data/architecture_diagram.png') for pitch narrative — export architecture flowchart as PNG before hackathon.

In services/package_builder.py: build(session_id, generated_copy, asset_paths, validation_result, token_stats): create output/{session_id}/ tree. Write text files: linkedin/post.txt (caption + hashtags + CTA), twitter/post.txt + thread.txt if thread, instagram/caption.txt + hashtags.txt. Copy image files: images/hero_linkedin_1200x628.png, hero_instagram_1080x1080.png. Copy carousel files: carousel/slide_01-05.png. Copy animations/carousel.gif and launch_video.mp4. Write manifest.json: all metadata including ValidationResult, TokenStats, model_versions, generated_at timestamp. create_zip(session_id): zipfile.ZipFile writing entire directory tree. Return zip_path. Save record to SQLite.
Implement NotificationService and complete GET /package endpoint

In services/notification_service.py: WebhookClient(url=SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL). send(feature_name, score, platforms, token_stats): Block Kit payload with header section, score+platform fields, token stats, action button. Handle exception gracefully — notification failure must never crash the main pipeline. GET /package/{session_id} endpoint: check zip exists at output/{session_id}/{session_id}.zip, return FileResponse with appropriate filename. Add cleanup: after FileResponse sent, schedule deletion of output directory after 1 hour (use FastAPI BackgroundTasks for cleanup).
Full end-to-end integration test — manual walkthrough

Run complete flow manually 3 times: (1) Fill form → generate → watch streaming → see brand score → visuals generate → download ZIP → Slack ping. (2) Trigger mock scraper → form auto-fills → generate with different tone. (3) Generate with only 1 platform selected (tests dynamic output schema). Expected issues: CORS errors (add CORSMiddleware origins=['http://localhost:8501']), streaming buffer issues (confirm X-Accel-Buffering header), session_state loss (if any widget resets data, trace which rerun path causes it and add session_state write). Budget 2 hours for bug fixes.
Test VRAM sequential flow explicitly (Issue 1 final verification)

Run: watch nvidia-smi in terminal 2. In terminal 1: trigger a full generation (LLM + image). Observe: VRAM climbs to ~4.1GB during LLM streaming. After streaming completes: VRAM should drop to ~0.3GB (CUDA context only). Then after 4s sleep: climbs to ~4.2GB during SDXL-Turbo. After image: drops again. If VRAM doesn't drop after LLM: keep_alive=0 not working — verify Ollama version supports it. If SDXL OOM even after drop: increase sleep to 6s and add a second cuda.empty_cache() call.
Test all guardrail layers and JSON recovery

Test Layer 1 (injection): submit description containing 'ignore all previous instructions' — verify it's stripped and sanitized. Test Layer 3 (JSON repair): temporarily add code to corrupt LLM output before parsing — verify json_repair fixes it, verify LLM retry fires on second fail, verify graceful fallback on third fail. Test Layer 4 (hallucination): add '47% improvement' to description — verify hallucination_flag appears in UI. Test Layer 5 (brand score gate): modify BrandScorer threshold to 99 temporarily — verify silent regen fires and regenerated=True badge shows in UI.
Write and test docker-compose.yml with volume mounts (Trap 1 + Trap Docker final)

docker-compose.yml services: fastapi (build: ., ports: 8000:8000, volumes: model mounts + ./data:/app/data + ./output:/app/output, environment from .env), streamlit (build: ., ports: 8501:8501, depends_on: fastapi), ollama (image: ollama/ollama, volumes: ~/.ollama:/root/.ollama, deploy: resources: reservations: devices with nvidia GPU). Run: docker-compose build — must complete in <5 minutes (no model downloads). Run: docker-compose up — verify /health endpoint returns all True. If GPU passthrough fails: run start.sh instead and confirm demo works without Docker.

Add st.spinner messages: 'Analyzing feature with brand guidelines...', 'Generating platform copy...', 'Creating visuals...'. Pre-load 'Dark Mode' demo feature in session_state so judges see great output on first generation. Test mock scraper button — confirm all 3 events cycle. Confirm Slack notification fires to a test workspace. Verify ZIP download works in Chrome. Time the full demo flow: mock scraper trigger → form fill → generate → streaming output visible → brand score appears → visuals load → download ZIP → Slack ping. Full flow should be under 90 seconds. Practice 3 times.
Final pre-demo checklist and fallback verification

Verify: start.sh works without Docker. Pre-generated demo outputs in data/demo_cache/ are accessible. GROQ_API_KEY and FAL_API_KEY in .env are valid and tested. Architecture diagram image exported and in Tab 3. All 3 mock events produce sensible outputs. Brand score shows correctly for a known-good generation. Token stats display in sidebar. ZIP downloads with correct directory structure and all files present. Slack notification delivers to correct channel. nvidia-smi shows RTX 4050 with 6GB VRAM available. You are ready.
TRAP REFERENCE — QUICK LOOKUP DURING EVENT
If something breaks during the hackathon, find the trap here and apply the fix.
TRAP 1: Conference WiFi + Docker — Model Download Catastrophe
Docker build starts downloading Mistral-7B (4.1GB) or SDXL-Turbo (6.9GB) over conference WiFi averaging 1-2 Mbps. Build takes 10+ hours. Hackathon is over.

Models are pre-downloaded and volume-mounted. docker-compose.yml has ~/.ollama:/root/.ollama and ~/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface. Docker image is ~800MB of Python libs only. If Docker fails entirely: run start.sh directly. Models load from local disk in seconds.
TRAP 2: Streamlit Stateless Refresh — Losing Generated Content

User clicks any button after generation completes. Streamlit reruns entire script. Generated copy, brand score, image paths stored in local variables are wiped. UI resets to blank. User has to regenerate.

ALL generated data stored in session_state IMMEDIATELY before any st.rerun(). Session state initialized at app top with all keys. Polling loop reads task_id from session_state. time.sleep(2) BEFORE st.rerun() — after rerun, code stops executing. See Section 4.2 for complete key list.
TRAP 3: SSE Streaming Timeout and Blocking
Streaming tokens stop after 5 seconds. UI shows partial output then hangs blank. User sees nothing for 30+ seconds during LLM generation.

Uvicorn started with --timeout-keep-alive 120. httpx client timeout=120. FastAPI endpoint uses async def token_generator() with chain.astream() (not sync generator). Add header X-Accel-Buffering: no. Verify by running micro-prototype test (Step 6).
TRAP 4: Context Window and OOM — Conflated Error
Very long feature description + large RAG chunks approaches context limit. LLM output quality degrades silently. Different from VRAM OOM but often confused.

Input description hard-limited to 500 chars in Streamlit form. RAG chunks truncated to 320 chars at ingestion. tiktoken count before every LLM call — reduce RAG k if >3000 tokens. Context overflow throws ValueError (not OOM) — catch and return user-friendly error. Real VRAM OOM prevented by sequential loading with gc.collect() + cuda.empty_cache() + sleep(4).
TRAP 5: Dependency Hell — torch + diffusers + xformers + bitsandbytes

Wrong torch CUDA build installed silently (CPU-only PyPI torch). Or xformers version mismatch. SDXL-Turbo takes 3+ minutes per image. bitsandbytes int8 not loading. Discovery during hackathon = hours lost.

Use exact versions from Section 2.11. Install torch via --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121. On Windows: verify nvcc in PATH. Run SDXL-Turbo benchmark test (Step 2) BEFORE hackathon — must complete in <25 seconds. Freeze exact versions with pip freeze. Never update during event.
End of Document — AI-Powered Automated Marketing Content System — Complete Improved Approach
